data.title: As an operator, I can configure PR comment response to propose each classification before acting when human review is preferred
data.description:
## Description

**What**: A configurable autonomy knob for the /dso:respond-to-pr-comments skill, controlled by a new pr_comments.human_in_loop key in dso-config.conf. When enabled, the skill proposes each classification and waits for confirmation before dispatching the handler.
**Why**: Some teams prefer to review proposed comment responses before they execute. This story adds an optional confirmation layer with safe CI/TTY fallbacks so the feature never deadlocks in automated environments.
**Scope**:
- IN: New pr_comments.human_in_loop key in dso-config.conf (default: false; absent key = false); when true, skill presents each comment's proposed classification and waits for user confirmation before calling the handler; CI detection (CI=true env var) and no-TTY detection — when either is true, fall back to autonomous mode and emit a warning message; config key namespace note: pr_comments.human_in_loop may be superseded by 7ac3-71a1 generalized pattern if that epic ships first; coordinate key naming to avoid conflict
- OUT: The generalized human-in-loop pattern (deferred to 7ac3-71a1); any other config keys or autonomy controls; classification logic (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, setting pr_comments.human_in_loop=true in dso-config.conf causes /dso:respond-to-pr-comments to display each proposed classification and wait for user confirmation before dispatching the handler
  ← Satisfies: "when true, proposes each classification before acting" (SC7)
- When this story is complete, when running with CI=true environment variable or without an interactive TTY, the skill falls back to autonomous mode and emits a warning (does not deadlock or fail silently)
  ← Satisfies: "detects CI=true or no TTY and falls back to autonomous with a warning" (SC7)
- When this story is complete, a missing pr_comments.human_in_loop key in dso-config.conf behaves identically to false (fully autonomous mode)
  ← Satisfies: "default: false; absent key = false" (SC7)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Reliability] TTY detection must fire BEFORE displaying any confirmation prompt to prevent deadlock in non-interactive environments; test must cover the CI-environment fallback path explicitly
- [Maintainability] Code comment noting that this config key may be superseded by 7ac3-71a1 generalized pattern; coordinate with that epic's namespace to avoid duplicate key names

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
